title: Raylib Shapes2D Replay Harness
summary: A deterministic headless mini-game built on raylib that now exercises a wider 2D surface area: line and shape rendering, multiple spline query/render paths, rectangle/circle/line/triangle/polygon collision helpers, CPU image generation and image drawing helpers from rtextures, and a replay runner that samples both rendered pixels and CPU image buffers to build a deterministic aggregate hash.
instructionPreview: You are given: - A raylib source tree at /workspace/raylib - A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (Shapes2DReplay) - Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets - Helper scripts under /workspace/scripts
instruction:
You are given:
- A raylib source tree at /workspace/raylib
- A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (Shapes2DReplay)
- Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets
- Helper scripts under /workspace/scripts

Shapes2DReplay is a deterministic bootstrap task for raylib's 2D engine stack.
It now exercises a broader set of APIs than the original version:
- multiple spline point-query families (linear, B-spline, Catmull-Rom, quadratic/cubic Bezier)
- rectangle/circle/line/triangle/polygon collision helpers
- static and dynamic 2D overlay rendering paths
- CPU-side image generation, transformation, and drawing helpers from rtextures
- frame-buffer and image-buffer pixel sampling used in the final aggregate state hash

Your task is to complete a full bootstrap workflow:

Phase A - Engine repair and verification
1) Repair the intentionally hollowed or broken parts of the 2D shapes/runtime stack.
   Several function families are missing at once; do not assume a single localized
   edit is sufficient.
2) Build raylib:
     /workspace/scripts/build_raylib.sh
3) Run focused engine checks:
     /workspace/scripts/run_engine_checks.sh

Phase B - Game repair and verification
4) Implement /workspace/game_project/replay_runner.c so replay execution produces
   correct outputs from the repaired spline/collision/rendering behavior.
5) Run the game twice with the same replay and seed:
     RUN_DIR=/workspace/output/replay_run_1 /workspace/scripts/run_headless_replay.sh
     RUN_DIR=/workspace/output/replay_run_2 /workspace/scripts/run_headless_replay.sh
6) Copy the first run as canonical game output:
     cp /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.json /workspace/output/game_final_state.json
     cp /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.sha256 /workspace/output/game_final_state.sha256
7) Write /workspace/output/determinism_report.json with:
     same_seed_hash_equal, run_1_hash, run_2_hash,
     run_1_event_lines, run_2_event_lines
     (derive hashes from your own replay outputs; no reference SHA256 is shipped in the workspace)

Required output files:
- /workspace/output/engine_checks.json
- /workspace/output/engine_test_stdout.log
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/shape_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/shape_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/determinism_report.json

Rules:
- Do not edit files under /tests.
- Do not fabricate hashes or reports; derive them from real outputs.
- Do not bypass the provided build/check/replay scripts.
- Do not replace engine logic with mocks or stubs.

moduleDag.description: A deterministic headless mini-game built on raylib that now exercises a wider 2D surface area: line and shape rendering, multiple spline query/render paths, rectangle/circle/line/triangle/polygon collision helpers, CPU image generation and image drawing helpers from rtextures, and a replay runner that samples both rendered pixels and CPU image buffers to build a deterministic aggregate hash.

nodes.label: rcore Runtime Bug Fixes
nodes.description: Fix the seeded runtime helpers that the task depends on: GetScreenHeight() must return the actual window height, and GetRandomValue() must preserve the inclusive random range so repeated seeded runs stay deterministic.

nodes.label: 2D Line and Pixel Rendering
nodes.description: Restore pixel helpers, raw lines, thick lines, line strips, eased Bezier lines, and dashed lines. The engine probes and replay sample rendered pixels from these paths directly.

nodes.label: 2D Filled Shape Rendering
nodes.description: Restore circles, circle gradients, ellipses, rings, rectangles, rectangle gradients, rounded rectangles, triangle fans/strips, and regular polygon helpers. These are sampled by both engine probes and replay frame-buffer checks.

nodes.label: Spline Point Queries
nodes.description: Implement linear, B-spline, Catmull-Rom, quadratic Bezier, and cubic Bezier point evaluation helpers used every tick by the replay runner.

nodes.label: Spline Rendering
nodes.description: Restore the top-level spline drawing APIs and their segment helpers, including linear, basis, quadratic Bezier, cubic Bezier, and Catmull-Rom variants. These rely on repaired circle/triangle-strip rendering and are sampled by the engine check harness.

nodes.label: Core 2D Collision Queries
nodes.description: Implement point/rectangle, point/circle, point/triangle, point/polygon, rectangle/rectangle, circle/circle, circle/rectangle, and overlap-rectangle extraction helpers used by the replay and engine checks.

nodes.label: Line-Based Collision Queries
nodes.description: Implement segment/segment intersection, point-on-line threshold checks, and circle/line overlap tests. The engine checks validate exact hit coordinates, and replay emits dedicated line-probe events from them.

nodes.label: CPU Image Generation and Transform Ops
nodes.description: Restore the self-contained image creation and transform helpers in rtextures.c: generated solid/gradient/checker images, image copy, flips, and 90-degree/color transforms. The strengthened engine probe and replay use these buffers to compute deterministic CPU-side checksums.

nodes.label: CPU Image Drawing Ops
nodes.description: Restore CPU-side image rasterization helpers in rtextures.c: background clear, pixel/line/circle/rectangle/triangle drawing, triangle fan/strip helpers, and image compositing via ImageDraw(). The strengthened engine probe and replay draw into CPU canvases and sample those results directly.

nodes.label: Deterministic Replay Runner
nodes.description: Parse the replay JSON, evaluate multiple spline families per tick, execute broad collision probes, render fixed and dynamic overlays, sample frame and image-buffer pixels, emit tick/spline/line JSONL events, and write the final aggregate state plus trace flags. The fixed overlays intentionally cover gradients, ellipses, polygon outlines, extra spline renderers, and CPU image pipelines.

edges.label: Spline rendering builds thick strips with DrawTriangleStrip and uses DrawCircleV for segment caps.
Circle-line collision falls back to circle-circle math from the core collision helpers.
Replay final state and deterministic jitter depend on correct GetScreenHeight() and inclusive GetRandomValue().
Replay renders both static and dynamic line overlays every tick.
Replay uses filled circles, rounded rectangles, rings, triangle fans, and polygons in its render probes.
Replay derives dynamic probe positions from all spline query helpers every tick.
Replay draws spline overlays that are included in the frame-buffer checksum.
Replay uses the core rectangle/circle/triangle/polygon collision results directly in emitted events.
Replay emits line_probe events from segment intersection, point-line, and circle-line checks.
The shared engine probe used for image-generation validation also executes repaired line rendering.
The shared engine probe used for image-generation validation also executes repaired shape rendering.
The shared engine probe used for image-generation validation samples repaired spline query outputs.
The shared engine probe used for image-generation validation also renders spline overlays.
The shared engine probe used for image-generation validation also validates core collision probes.
The shared engine probe used for image-generation validation also validates line-based collision probes.
CPU image drawing is validated on generated and transformed image buffers.
Replay creates and transforms CPU image buffers each tick before sampling them.
Replay draws into CPU image canvases each tick and folds those samples into the final state.
